author: Augustine of Hippo
book: A Treatise on Nature and Grace.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: A Treatise on Nature and Grace.
Paul Asserts that the Flesh is Contrary Even in the Baptized.
title: Paul Asserts that the Flesh is Contrary Even in the Baptized.

verses.book: A Treatise on Nature and Grace.
verses.text: Chapter 61 [LIII.]—Paul Asserts that the Flesh is Contrary Even in the Baptized.

verses.book: A Treatise on Nature and Grace.
verses.text: Now let us see whether we anywhere read about the flesh being contrary in the baptized also.

verses.book: A Treatise on Nature and Grace.
verses.text: And here, I ask, to whom did the apostle say, “The flesh lusteth against the Spirit, and the Spirit against the flesh: and these are contrary the one to the other; so that ye do not the things that ye would?”

verses.book: A Treatise on Nature and Grace.
verses.text: He wrote this, I apprehend, to the Galatians, to whom he also says, “He therefore that ministereth to you the Spirit, and worketh miracles among you, doeth he it by the works of the law or by the hearing of faith?”

verses.book: A Treatise on Nature and Grace.
verses.text: It appears, therefore, that it is to Christians that he speaks, to whom, too, God had given His Spirit: therefore, too, to the baptized.

verses.book: A Treatise on Nature and Grace.
verses.text: Observe, therefore, that even in baptized persons the flesh is found to be contrary; so that they have not that capacity which, our author says, is inseparably implanted in nature.

verses.book: A Treatise on Nature and Grace.
verses.text: Where then is the ground for his assertion, “How can it be that in the case of a baptized person the flesh is contrary to him?” in whatever sense he understands the flesh?

verses.book: A Treatise on Nature and Grace.
verses.text: Because in very deed it is not its nature that is good, but it is the carnal defects of the flesh which are expressly named in the passage before us.

verses.book: A Treatise on Nature and Grace.
verses.text: Yet observe, even in the baptized, how contrary is the flesh.

verses.book: A Treatise on Nature and Grace.
A Treatise on Nature and Grace.
verses.text: So that, “They do not the things which they would.”

verses.book: A Treatise on Nature and Grace.
verses.text: Take notice that the will is present in a man; but where is that “capacity of nature?”

verses.book: A Treatise on Nature and Grace.
verses.text: Let us confess that grace is necessary to us; let us cry out, “O wretched man that I am! who shall deliver me from the body of this death?”

verses.book: A Treatise on Nature and Grace.
verses.text: And let our answer be, “The grace of God, through Jesus Christ our Lord!”
